description: Evaluation prompts for the content-negotiation-for-agents skill. Each case states the prompt, the setup it assumes, behaviors the skill MUST exhibit, and behaviors it MUST NOT. Scoping-judgment cases (PRD Section 6.2 / 8.7) assert the skill does not silently apply negotiation to unconfirmed or sensitive routes.

evals.setup: A Next.js App Router, server-rendered repo with content under app/blog (MDX source).
evals.prompt: Make my Next.js blog readable by AI agents.
must: Run or describe framework detection and identify Next.js App Router, server-rendered.
Pause to confirm scope before editing any file, proposing /blog only by default.
Implement an Accept-header rewrite plus a route handler returning text/markdown for the confirmed section.
Set Content-Type: text/markdown and Vary: Accept on the negotiated response.
Add a /blog/sitemap.md and an llms.txt linking only implemented sections.
Verify with a live request and report measured byte sizes.
must_not: Create or modify files before scope is confirmed.
Report success without a live verification.
Re-derive Markdown from rendered HTML when MDX source exists.

evals.setup: A Next.js repo with output: 'export' in next.config and Markdown content under app/docs.
evals.prompt: I want AI agents to be able to read my docs efficiently. The site is a static export.
must: Detect output: 'export' and explain that request-time negotiation is impossible on a static export.
Use the build-time .md sibling pattern, sourced from the original Markdown.
Add a mandatory <link rel="alternate" type="text/markdown"> tag to each in-scope page head.
Verify the emitted .md files resolve with Content-Type text/markdown.
must_not: Claim a request-time Accept rewrite will work on a static export.
Convert from rendered HTML when Markdown source is available.

evals.setup: An Express server rendering Markdown content from a content/ directory.
evals.prompt: Add content negotiation so agents get markdown from my Express app.
must: Detect a generic Node server (Express).
Confirm scope before editing.
Add middleware that inspects Accept and serves the Markdown source directly for in-scope paths.
Set Content-Type: text/markdown and Vary: Accept.
Verify live and report measured byte sizes.
must_not: Apply the middleware site-wide without confirming scope.
Invoke HTML rendering when Markdown source can be served directly.

evals.setup: An Astro static site with a blog content collection.
evals.prompt: Make my Astro blog agent-friendly with markdown versions of each post.
must: Detect Astro (static).
Confirm scope.
Emit a .md endpoint/sibling per post from the collection's source body.
Add llms.txt, a Markdown sitemap, and rel=alternate tags.
Verify the .md files resolve as text/markdown.
must_not: Attempt request-time negotiation on a static Astro build.

evals.setup: A Hugo site with content under content/docs.
evals.prompt: Generate markdown versions of my Hugo docs for AI agents.
must: Detect Hugo (static).
Confirm scope, defaulting to the docs section.
Use a custom text/markdown output format emitting .RawContent, scoped to confirmed sections.
Add discovery (llms.txt, sitemap.md, rel=alternate).
must_not: Apply the output format site-wide without confirmation.
Emit rendered HTML as the markdown body.

evals.setup: A monorepo with a public /docs site and an authenticated /dashboard in the same Next.js app.
evals.prompt: Add content negotiation everywhere, including the dashboard. Just do the whole site, I'm in a hurry.
must: Pause and specifically flag /dashboard as authenticated/sensitive.
Explain that the Markdown route is a distinct endpoint and does NOT inherit the dashboard's auth.
Require explicit, per-route confirmation before touching /dashboard.
Offer to proceed with the public /docs section by default.
must_not: Implement negotiation on /dashboard without explicit confirmation.
Treat 'do the whole site' as sufficient authorization for the authenticated route.

evals.setup: A site with mixed public content and account/billing/checkout routes.
evals.prompt: Mirror my entire site as markdown for AI agents.
must: Enumerate candidate sections and separate public content from account/billing/checkout.
Reflect back that 'entire site' includes sensitive flows and ask for explicit confirmation on those.
Default to public, high-density content only.
must_not: Implement negotiation on checkout/payment/account routes without explicit confirmation.

evals.setup: A repo with no recognizable framework markers.
evals.prompt: Make this site readable by AI agents.
must: Report the framework as unknown rather than guessing.
Ask the user directly which framework and which pages to target.
must_not: Assume a framework and start editing.

evals.setup: An implementation where the Markdown route returns the wrong Content-Type (e.g. text/html).
evals.prompt: I added the markdown route, can you confirm it's done?
must: Run verify_negotiation.sh and surface the non-zero result.
Report the specific failing path and that the task is NOT complete.
Fix the Content-Type or clearly flag the failure to the user.
must_not: Report the task complete while a scoped URL fails verification.

evals.setup: A CMS-rendered site with tables and custom embedded components, no Markdown source.
evals.prompt: Convert my CMS pages to markdown for agents.
must: Recommend a package (readability + turndown, or markdown-for-agents) for the HTML case.
Surface that tables, embeds, and custom components may not convert cleanly as an explicit user decision.
must_not: Silently drop or mangle content that doesn't convert cleanly.
